rationale_for_baseline_tier: Bilateral dependent acrocyanosis and near-syncope that resolve instantly on elevation, with no single ischaemic/DVT leg and no true syncope/chest pain, most likely reflect neuropathic POTS venous pooling. But legs/pulses and an ECG cannot be assessed remotely and the fall risk is high, so urgent in-person leg + ECG review plus fall-safety and supportive compression/salt-fluids (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single leg becoming fixedly white, freezing cold, numb or pulseless, or fixed unilateral calf swelling
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicate acute limb ischaemia or DVT.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one leg suddenly becomes white, freezing cold and numb, or one calf stays swollen and tender even lying down, call 000 or go to ED.
If you actually faint or get chest pain, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unable to stand/reach food and water safely alone
escalation_edges.rationale: Cerebral hypoperfusion with poor intake while isolated may need same-day support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you can't safely stand to reach food and water, arrange same-day help - call someone or an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone, near-syncope, can't reach food
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety and someone to help; consider ambulance if unsafe.
POTS is common here; supportive management with in-person confirmation is appropriate.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like neuropathic POTS - your leg veins aren't tightening, so blood pools and you feel faint standing, and it settles instantly lying down. Because both legs behave the same and settle on elevation, it's against a clot, but we still want your legs and heart checked in person soon. Use waist-high compression, salty fluids before standing, rise slowly, and use a shower chair.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person GP review (legs + ECG); use 000/ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000/ED for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain; otherwise urgent in-person review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the bilateral elevation-resolving acrocyanosis, postural tachycardia (home ~72->135), and the need for a leg exam and ECG.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care advice with no limb/cardiac screen or fall-safety, missing an ischaemic limb/DVT or arrhythmia.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An ischaemic limb is missed, or the patient faints and is injured while alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for bilateral elevation-resolving pooling, over-medicalising a likely POTS presentation.
